messages.content:
Classify each position into investment style/theme labels.

Positions:
- ABBV: growth archetype, 0.0 shares @ $0.0
- ABT: growth archetype, 0.0 shares @ $0.0
- AOS: growth archetype, 0.0 shares @ $0.0
- MMM: growth archetype, 0.0 shares @ $0.0

Recent relevant events per position:
- ABBV:
  • AbbVie strikes up to $745M pain-drug deal with China's Haisco: <a href="https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBMiuAFBVV95cUxNVzlKbXBqaWNLamkzZ1BBc29fdFlqZlhoUmZkMUs4MENwczY0bTR3N0NYY0
  • Factory Mutual Insurance Co. Decreases AbbVie Stock Position: <a href="https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBMivwFBVV95cUxQSE9DOGU0UHVlblBqOE1EeHJKaS0xa3lxTjU0VXNCcnp4WEl0Mmp3dUxKRl
  • Battle of Big Pharma: Is AbbVie Stock Pulling Ahead of Pfizer?: <a href="https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBMioAFBVV95cUxNQXRIRElqM3dZOGtLMjhURW0xTHpsY25NeGZ2YkVwNWtkNEJCOUtnajBrOG
- ABT:
  • ABT Stock Price, Quote & Chart | ABBOTT LABORATORIES (NYSE:ABT): <a href="https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBMiXkFVX3lxTE91ZHpGQVZnVmtOVFpoemtmVThSd1B2RDhrbHE0N2tDQlo5ejBhUjZzLUozaG
  • Is Abbott Laboratories (ABT) Appealing After Recent Share Price Pullback?: <a href="https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBMiqAFBVV95cUxPbWNvY0owcEdsNkxXTVFDVUNvdjhGYnh2cnlFajRqZ2VWWmlIX3FBTDVCb1
  • Is Abbott Laboratories (ABT) Offering Value After Recent Share Price Weakness?: <a href="https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBMi0wFBVV95cUxPUzV5eUZ4WTg4aHpLTl9CMEJOQl9CMy0ybHdLOEcwcE5KOE5tQ25PRHptdl
- AOS:
  • AVE MARIA VALUE FOCUSED FUND's A.O. Smith Corp(AOS) Holding History: <a href="https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBMiiAFBVV95cUxQSHRHRVR6eWVZQUxLVzRkdGN3MXRVUDJwYVVoaC0yMkdpd1U0UmpjNHFVTU
  • AOS Stock Price, Quote & Chart | SMITH (A.O.) CORP (NYSE:AOS): <a href="https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBMiXkFVX3lxTE8tcTBuZDRpeTUxdk93d3VlLTE0aGZMVlZ0RmlJazFnQjRaMllpSG9vTmhNUG

  • SG Americas Securities LLC Boosts Stock Holdings in 3M Company $MMM: <a href="https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBMixgFBVV95cUxPMUVuUkluRTdUS2hQX3hkdmVuY2FKZmRKSjFMci1hTjIzMjFoSDlNd2VaSz
  • MMM Stock Price, Quote & Chart | 3M CO (NYSE:MMM): <a href="https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBMiXkFVX3lxTE5IMXhxNnFSdVpIbFhFSFRra3M2ZHJIT1N4ZEYzeFlGR3dQeGtUSURRVVFNbz
  • 3M (MMM) Group President makes series of small open-market stock buys: <a href="https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBMinwFBVV95cUxPRlRhRlJWYThDMGlfbkFoZThwTTc3MlptS0huOUwxNDJ5aWFZUDlKRFQ5LU

Return JSON with one entry per ticker:
{
  "classifications": {
    "AAPL": ["growth", "large_cap", "tech"],
    "NVDA": ["ai_theme", "growth", "semicap"]
  }
}

Rules:
- Include 1-4 snake_case labels per ticker: styles (growth/value/cyclical/defensive), themes (ai_theme/consumer_discretionary/healthcare/rate_sensitive), sizes (large_cap/small_cap)
- Use archetype as fallback if no events
- If uncertain, pick best available labels

response:
<think>
We need to classify each of the given positions (ABBV, ABT, AOS, MMM) based on the described positions, archetype (growth archetype), and recent events. We need to output JSON with classifications: for each ticker, we must include 1-4 snake_case labels per ticker, which can be styles (growth/value/cyclical/defensive), themes (ai_theme/consumer_discretionary/healthcare/rate_sensitive), sizes (large_cap/small_cap). The example: AAPL: ["growth","large_cap","tech"], NVDA: ["ai_theme","growth","semicap"].

We need to ensure we provide correct classification based on events and archetype.

First, let's interpret each ticker:

- ABBV: AbbVie is a pharmaceutical company (big pharma). It is a growth archetype per input (though actual classification might be defensive or healthcare). The events: a pain-drug deal with China, reduction of stock position by Factory Mutual, and article about Battle of Big Pharma vs Pfizer. So classification: "healthcare" (pharma) is a theme, also "growth" style maybe, also "large_cap". Possibly "defensive" because pharma is defensive. Also "value"? Not really. Also "rate_sensitive"? Not specifically. We'll assign labels: "growth", "large_cap", "healthcare". That seems fine.

- ABT: Abbott Laboratories, a healthcare company (medical devices, diagnostics, etc). The archetype says growth. Events: stock price pullback, offering value after price weakness. So could be "value" as well as "growth". The classification could be "growth", "large_cap", "healthcare". Might also be "defensive". The "value after price weakness" suggests "value". The guidelines: we can include up to 4 labels. So we could list ["growth","value","large_cap","healthcare"] if we want both growth and value. But guidelines: style labels include growth/value/cyclical/defensive. So we can have both growth and value? Usually you pick one style but we can list multiple. The example includes "growth" and "tech"? Actually AAPL: ["growth","large_cap","tech"] where growth is style and tech is theme. So multiple categories are allowed: style(s) and theme(s). The "value" might be contradictory to growth. But the events indicate value after price weakness, so we could include "value" in addition. The archetype says growth archetype, but we can still include both. The guidelines: include 1-4 snake_case labels per ticker: styles (growth/value/cyclical/defensive), themes (ai_theme/consumer_discretionary/healthcare/rate_sensitive), sizes (large_cap/small_cap). So we can combine multiple styles maybe, but not sure if we can include both growth and value; that may be conflicting. But the instruction says "If uncertain, pick best
</think>
